bibliography.title: Photo-physiological costs associated with acute sediment stress events in three near-shore turbid water corals

exposure.sediment_composition: Silicon carbide powder proxy for natural reef sediment (particle sizes 1ŌĆō300 ┬Ąm; median 60 ┬Ąm).
exposure.sediment_level_uom: mg lŌłÆ1 (turbidity target for Treatment 2: 102.4 mg lŌłÆ1 ┬▒ 13.9)
exposure.notes: Acute sediment resuspension stress event; corals exposed to sediment Treatment 2 in Week 2 for 2 h (air bursts at 0, 30, 60, 90 min during Weeks 2ŌĆō4). Evidence: "sediment Treatment 2 (turbidity = 102.4 mg lŌłÆ1 ┬▒ 13.9; sedimentation rate = 4.3 ┬▒ 0.4 mg cmŌłÆ2 hŌłÆ1" and "Each acute sediment stress event lasted for 2 h".

warnings: Only Table 1 treatment rows (Treatment 2ŌĆō4) were numerically extractable from the provided text image snippet; Treatment 1 baseline numeric values were present but not included because setup definition here requires unique combinations of genus/species and sediment exposure duration/level. Baseline corresponds to no sediment event (air burst only) and is a control; however baseline turbidity/sedimentation rate values were not provided in the snippet, so sediment-level setup fields cannot be populated reliably for Treatment 1 from supplied text.

Table 1 formatting for Treatment 2 net photosynthesis shows ambiguous spacing for Pachyseris ("0.36  0.47 (0.15)"), but 0.36 was extracted for Pachyseris net photosynthesis as the first displayed value in that row for that species.
